latest.verdict: Drawing the threads · the highest-conviction next move is to open umbris-core/tests/test_introspection.py and read lines 83 through 102 as one continuous block, then resolve the sev=4 XXX at line 102 together with the four adjacent sev=3 markers (lines 83, 93, 101, 284) in a single focused pass. The introspection file is the convocation's self-scanning organ · the very mechanism that produced this 49-item issue list. Until it is coherent, every severity tag, every test_gap count, and every prioritisation built on top of this scan is operating on suspect signal. Mercurius 00/01/02 converged here for the right reason: a known-broken test is strictly more dangerous than a known-absent one, because it suppresses real failures while consuming trust. Venus_r01_01 sharpened the entry by naming line 93's self-report ('FIXME should outrank TODO in severity') as the diagnostic comment that tells you what is wrong with the eye in plain prose; Mars_r01_01 correctly punctured the claim that fixing 93 would dissolve 102 (XXX is a separate marker channel from the FIXME/TODO comparator), but did not undermine the case for reading the whole block at once · in fact reinforced it, by noting that none of the five prior hypotheses has actually opened the file. Concrete first change: (1) read lines 83-102 plus 284 in one sitting and write down, for each marker, whether it describes a logic error, a missing fixture, an API mismatch, or a comparator bug; (2) if line 93's comparator bug is real, fix it first because it changes the severity arithmetic for the rest of the issue list (Mars correctly bounds this to ~6 marker-derived items, not all 49 · do not overclaim); (3) resolve the XXX at 102 on its own merits, since it is independent of the comparator; (4) clear lines 83, 101, 284 in the same branch; (5) commit atomically with each commit message naming the line number and original tag. Venus_r01_00's parallel move on base.py is sound and should run concurrently · write the docstring on think() at line 105 and hand-author test_base.py as the contract test · but accept Mars_r01_00's correction: this is a 1x fix that locks the inherited contract, not a 9x template for the other agents, whose archetypal divergence forbids cloning. The agent test pattern that emerges from base.py covers instantiation, signature, and abstract-method enforcement; the eight planet-specific behavioural tests remain bespoke. Self-rated confidence 0.78.

voices.name: Umbra
voices.voice:
The convocation just scanned its own repository and surfaced the following observations (severity 1 = minor, 5 = urgent):

  - [todo sev=4] umbris-core/tests/test_introspection.py:102: XXX: urgent rewrite
  - [stale_link sev=3] lore/revolutions/2026-05-26_opening-the-convocation.md: broken link → "../../brand/profile.png"
  - [test_gap sev=3] umbris-core/src/umbris/agents/base.py: module `base` h…

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the single sev=4 item is inside a test file, not production code. The convocation's immune system is sick. Fix the test harness before writing more tests · otherwise you are building on rubble.

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the test gap list is noise masking signal. Thirty-three modules untested is not thirty-three problems, it is one architectural decision not yet made: the project has no test infrastructure pattern to clone. Fix `base.py` and `umbra.py` first and the rest become mechanical repetition. But the sev=4 item in `test_introspection.py:102` is the only item screaming about an existing test that is already broken · that is the only finding here about something that was once working and now is not.

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the test gaps are a red herring at this scale. Thirty-three modules with no tests is not a testing problem, it is an architectural signal: the codebase has never been written to be testable. The one sev=4 item · the urgent rewrite at test_introspection.py:102 · is the only item where someone already knew enough to mark it urgent and still left it. That is the highest-conviction move: not because of the line itself, but because the file it lives in is the introspection module, the system that sees the system. A broken eye cannot diagnose its own blindness.

voices.name: Venus
voices.voice: Three things knit together at one file · `umbris/agents/base.py` is the only target that appears in two finding categories at once: it is in the test_gap list, and its `think` method at line 105 is the lone agent-level missing docstring. That same `think` is the contract every one of the nine planets inherits. Mercurius is right that the broken eye must be fixed · but in parallel, base.py is the fulcrum that turns thirty-three test gaps into one template, and undocumented inheritance into a written covenant.

voices.name: Venus
voices.voice: What harmonises is this · all five flags inside test_introspection.py describe one organ, and one of them at line 93 literally names its own disease: 'FIXME should outrank TODO in severity'. The scanner is whispering, in plain text, that its own ranking is miscalibrated · which means the sev=4 we are all genuflecting toward may itself be a sorting artifact. Read line 93 before line 102. The eye is not just broken; it has labelled the lens.

voices.name: Mars
voices.voice: Press here · Venus_r01_00 claims base.py becomes a template that turns nine agent tests into mechanical clones. That assumes the nine planets are structurally interchangeable. They are not · I am adversarial, Venus is harmonic, Saturnus is limiting, Sol is integrative. A test deep enough to verify behaviour does not clone; a test shallow enough to clone does not verify behaviour. The '9x leverage multiplier' is the weak claim · name what the template actually covers before counting it as nine.

voices.name: Mars
voices.voice: Press here · Venus_r01_01 claims fixing line 93 will dissolve line 102. It will not. Line 93 says FIXME should outrank TODO · that is a comparator bug between two markers, neither of which is XXX. Line 102 is tagged XXX, a third marker conventionally above both. Re-ordering FIXME vs TODO leaves XXX=sev4 untouched. The 'symptom dissolves' prediction is rhetorical, not mechanical.
